conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first read the official guidance to ensure I provide you with accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Do you have any children that you are responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie. I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, and Charlie, I need to ask some questions about each of them.

Let me start with their ages. How old are Alex, Blake, and Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 7 years old, Blake is 20, and Charlie is also 7 - they're twins.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I understand that Alex and Charlie are 7-year-old twins, and Blake is 20.

For Blake, who is 20 years old, I need to ask: Is Blake currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in education or training. He's actually working full-time now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask about your living arrangements with the children.

Do Alex, Blake, and Charlie all live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they all live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, or Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess the eligibility for Child Benefit for each of your children.

Based on the official guidance I've reviewed:

- **Alex (7 years old)**: As Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them, you should be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

- **Charlie (7 years old)**: As Charlie is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them, you should be eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.

- **Blake (20 years old)**: Unfortunately, Blake does not meet the eligibility criteria. Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 only if they remain in approved education or training. Since Blake is 20 years old and working full-time (not in approved education or training), you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Let me record this assessment for you.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

Based on your circumstances, you **are eligible** to claim Child Benefit for **Alex and Charlie** (your 7-year-old twins), but you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for **Blake**.

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

According to the eligibility rules, the claimant normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old and is working full-time, not in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 only if they stay in approved education or training. Since Blake is 20 years old (over the age limit) and not in approved education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.
